skill.name: google-image-api-skill
skill.description: Extract structured image data from Google Images via BrowserAct API. Search by keywords, filter by country and language, control scroll depth and result limits, and receive clean metadata including titles, thumbnails, source logos, and click-through URLs.
skill.summary: Extract structured image metadata from Google Images with country/language filtering
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 76 potential issues, but after semantic evaluation, all are false positives. The skill makes legitimate API calls to BrowserAct (api.browseract.com) to extract Google Images data. Environment access (BROWSERACT_API_KEY) is for authentication only, not exfiltration. External command detections in SKILL.md are markdown formatting syntax, not actual shell execution. No malicious intent detected.

high_findings.title: Environment Variable Access Pattern
high_findings.description: The skill reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment. This is legitimate credential handling for API authentication - the key is only used to authenticate with BrowserAct service, not exfiltrated.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Static analyzer detected env_access pattern but misidentified it as dangerous. The code reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY to authenticate with BrowserAct API - standard credential usage, not exfiltration.

medium_findings.title: Documentation Contains Command Examples
medium_findings.description: SKILL.md contains bash command examples with backtick syntax for formatting. The static analyzer detects backticks as shell execution patterns, but these are markdown formatting, not actual code execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Pattern detection triggered by backtick syntax in markdown documentation. The backticks are formatting for bash examples, not actual shell execution in the skill.

medium_findings.title: Network Requests to External API
medium_findings.description: The skill makes HTTP requests to api.browseract.com. This is legitimate API integration for Google Images data extraction - the network access is necessary for the skill's core functionality.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Network requests are to the legitimate BrowserAct API endpoint. No evidence of data exfiltration to third-party servers beyond the expected service provider.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded API URLs
low_findings.description: The script contains hardcoded API base URL and workflow template ID. These are configuration constants for the BrowserAct service, not sensitive credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: URLs are public API endpoints for BrowserAct service. Workflow template ID is a configuration constant, not a secret.

content.user_title: Extract Google Images data with country and language filtering
content.value_statement: Manual image research is slow and inconsistent. This skill automates Google Images data extraction with structured metadata including titles, thumbnails, source logos, and click-through URLs, filtered by country and language.
actual_capabilities: Search Google Images by keywords and return structured metadata
Filter results by country (US, GB, JP, FR, DE, and 5 others)
Filter results by language (English, Chinese, Japanese, Korean, and 4 others)
Control scroll depth to load more results (default: 5 scrolls)
Extract titles, thumbnails, source logos, and click-through URLs
Detect product-style listings and track competitor visual assets
limitations: Requires valid BrowserAct API key (BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable)
Execution takes several minutes due to automated browser operations
Results depend on BrowserAct service availability and rate limits

use_cases.title: Market research and competitor analysis
use_cases.description: Extract product images and visual trends from different countries to understand how competitors appear in local markets.

use_cases.title: Visual content sourcing for creative teams
use_cases.description: Find and gather images for specific keywords across multiple regions to support design and content creation workflows.

use_cases.title: SEO keyword visualization tracking
use_cases.description: Check what images rank for target keywords in different countries to inform SEO and content strategy.

prompt_templates.title: Basic image search
prompt_templates.prompt: Search Google Images for [keyword] and give me the results with titles and source links

prompt_templates.title: Localized search by country
prompt_templates.prompt: Find images for [keyword] in Japan (country: jp) and show me the results in Japanese

prompt_templates.title: Competitor visual monitoring
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract image data for [brand name] products from US and UK markets. I want to see thumbnails, titles, and click-through links for product listings.

prompt_templates.title: Bulk visual research
prompt_templates.prompt: Search for [topic] images across US, Germany, France, and Japan. Return structured data with thumbnails, titles, and source logos. Extract up to 50 results per country.

output_examples.input: Search for 'electric vehicles' in Japan and show me the top 20 results
output_examples.output:
Found 20 image results for 'electric vehicles' in Japan (country: jp, language: ja):

1. Tesla Model 3 Charging - thumbnail available, source: tesla.com
2. BYD Electric SUV - thumbnail available, source: byd.com
3. Nissan Leaf 2024 - thumbnail available, source: nissan.co.jp

Total results: 20 | Products detected: 12 | Click-through links: Available

output_examples.input: Extract competitor product images for 'running shoes' from US and UK markets
output_examples.output:
Extracted competitor visual data for 'running shoes':

US Market (country: us):
- Nike Air Zoom (product listing detected)
- Adidas Ultraboost (product listing detected)
- New Balance Fresh Foam

UK Market (country: gb):
- Nike Air Max (product listing detected)
- Asics Gel-Kayano
- Under Armour HOVR

Total competitors tracked: 6 | Product listings: 4

best_practices: Set BROWSERACT_API_KEY as an environment variable before running the skill
Start with default parameters (5 scrolls, 50 results) before adjusting for larger datasets
Check for 'Invalid authorization' errors first - they indicate expired or invalid API keys
anti_patterns: Do not run multiple image extraction tasks simultaneously - the script polls for completion and may conflict
Do not ignore timeout errors - if the task takes longer than 5 minutes, check your BrowserAct dashboard
Do not skip API key validation - the skill will fail silently without proper authentication

faq.question: How do I get a BrowserAct API key?
faq.answer: Go to https://www.browseract.com/reception/integrations and copy your API key. Set it as the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable before running the skill.

faq.question: Why is the task taking so long?
faq.answer: The script uses automated browser operations which can take several minutes. The script will continuously output status logs like '[14:30:05] Task Status: running' while executing.

faq.question: What countries are supported?
faq.answer: Supported country codes include us, gb, ca, au, de, fr, es, jp, and kr. Use the country parameter to filter results by region.

faq.question: What languages are supported?
faq.answer: Supported languages include en (English), zh-CN (Simplified Chinese), zh-TW (Traditional Chinese), ja (Japanese), ko (Korean), fr (French), de (German), and es (Spanish).

faq.question: What data does the extraction return?
faq.answer: Results include is_product flag, click-through link, title, source_logo URL, source site name, thumbnail URL, related_content_id, and result index.

faq.question: How do I handle API key errors?
faq.answer: If you see 'Invalid authorization' in the output, do not retry. Instead, guide the user to check their BrowserAct API key at https://www.browseract.com/reception/integrations.
